evals.prompt: How do I safely unwrap optionals in Swift?
evals.expected_output: Use guard let, if let, or nil coalescing (??). Never force unwrap with !. Example: guard let value = optionalValue else { return }.

evals.prompt: What is the difference between protocol and class inheritance in Swift?
evals.expected_output: Protocols define a blueprint; classes inherit implementation. Prefer protocol composition with structs for better decoupling and performance. Classes are reference types; structs are value types.

evals.prompt: How do I use extension for protocol conformance?
evals.expected_output: Implement conformance in an extension to keep code clean: extension MyType: MyProtocol { ... }. Do not use extensions for stored properties.

evals.prompt: How do I use enums with associated values in Swift?
evals.expected_output: Define case with parameter: enum Result { case success(String), failure(Error) }. Extract via switch: case .success(let msg): print(msg). Better than multiple optionals.

evals.prompt: When should I use struct vs class in Swift?
evals.expected_output: Default to struct for value semantics and thread safety. Use class only when reference identity is necessary or for inheritance. Structs are copied; classes are shared.

should_not_trigger: How do I set up Fastlane?
How do I use Instruments for profiling?
